key_insight: A combined CEL simulation and ANN framework can predict and optimize friction stir welding quality in HDPE by linking process settings, thermal-mechanical response, and defect formation.
engineering_relevance: The study offers practical process maps for choosing rotational speed, travel speed, and preheating conditions to improve weld integrity and reduce wormhole defects in thermoplastic joining.
method: 3D coupled Eulerian-Lagrangian (CEL) model simulated temperature, axial force, material flow, and void formation under varied FSW settings.
Two multilayer perceptron ANNs were trained on experimental data to predict temporal temperature and axial force responses.
Experimental validation was used to compare simulated and measured weld morphology and process responses.
evidence_signal: The ANN models achieved high predictive performance with R2 values between 0.91 and 0.99, and the CEL model showed good agreement with experiments for thermal, mechanical, and defect outcomes.
watch_item: The optimized process window is specific to the tested HDPE setup and parameter range, so broader use should be validated for different tool geometries, thicknesses, and material grades.
paper_title: Computational and machine learning modelling approaches for weld quality predictions in friction stir welding of high-density polyethylene
